_comment: Buffalo Forge & Foundry brand config, derived from branding/Buffalo Forge Branding Page FINAL.png -- the Master Branding image (and the script logo cropped from branding/logo2.png). Colors/fonts/buttons are the guide's real values. Where the guide didn't specify something (status 'good', page-background tint, dark-mode steps), the derivation is noted per field.

fonts._comment: Three brand-specified roles. Email keeps one safe fallback stack per role (Arial/Helvetica) since condensed/geometric webfonts aren't reliably supported cross-client -- distinguish by weight/case there, not typeface.

body.web_font_stack: 'Source Sans Pro', system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif

colors._comment: surface/page/ink/accent are the guide's own named colors (section 4). Categorical is capped at 3 -- the dataviz skill's validator (scripts/validate_palette.js) confirmed Forge Orange + Molten Copper + Fire Gold pass CVD/lightness/chroma as a set, but adding the neutrals (black/gray/silver) fails the chroma-floor check (they read as gray, not as hued identity colors), and the brand simply has no 4th distinguishable hue. generate_chart.py already wraps the 3-color set with modulo and puts a direct value label on every mark, which is the validator-sanctioned relief for a floor-band palette.

dark._comment: page/primary_ink are the guide's own hero-banner black/white. surface is a lifted charcoal for card separation (not a named brand color). secondary_ink swaps to Steel Silver vs. light mode since Iron Gray doesn't clear contrast on a near-black page. Fire Gold's categorical step is darkened (validator-driven) to clear the dark lightness band -- #DB9B2D was too light at L 0.734.

status._comment: good/critical are the only tones the current templates actually render (stat_callout tone=positive/negative), so they're kept clean and distinct from the categorical set per the dataviz rule (status colors must never impersonate a series). Neither has a brand equivalent -- both are small new functional additions tuned to the industrial/warm mood. warning/serious are not wired into any template today, so they reuse Fire Gold and Molten Copper per the guide's own 'Callouts/Warnings' framing; revisit if a warning-tone UI element is ever added, since that would reintroduce the series/status collision.

buttons._comment: Section 7 of the brand guide, verbatim.
